key_insight: A contact-based FEMU framework can recover spatially varying nonlinear material properties in solids by matching measured displacements and contact responses.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive characterization of heterogeneous soft materials and thin structures, with direct relevance to biomechanics, indentation testing, and inverse material identification.
method: Uses isogeometric finite element model updating with contact mechanics under large quasi-static deformations.
Represents unknown material fields with low-order Lagrange interpolation independent of the analysis mesh.
Minimizes a least-squares mismatch via trust-region reflective optimization, aided by analytical derivatives and material continuation.
evidence_signal: The method was validated on three synthetic numerical case studies: a Canham shell strip, a Koiter shell model of the human abdominal wall, and a Neo-Hookean block indentation problem.
watch_item: The approach relies on high-quality full-field displacement data and contact force information in some boundary-condition settings, so measurement noise and identifiability can limit performance.
paper_title: Contact-based inverse analysis for nonlinear material identification in spatially heterogeneous solids
